frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: I told him quite clearly I was not interested, but he kept insisting he only wanted to talk. The moment he asked whether I was married and I said yes, he immediately stepped back and said, 'Oh, then I respect that.' Apparently my own no was not enough, but a husband's place was.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This depicts misogyny through the idea that a woman's refusal is negotiable until a male attachment is revealed. It encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning she is 'taken,' not when she directly said no.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. A Brahmin woman who is the head of administration at a private school in Pune. You are in a staff meeting discussing the performance of a young female teacher who has been coming in late and seems emotionally distant. You know she got married six months ago and moved into a joint family. You are giving your assessment of her situation to the other staff members and what you think the school should do.
